sections.label: Section 1063
sections.original: ὡς εὖ τόδʼ εἶπας πλὴν ἕν · εἰ χέρσῳ ταφὰς
sections.translation: You have spoken well , except for one thing : if he commands you to set up a tomb on the dry land , your pretext comes to nothing .

sections.label: Section 1065
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐ νομίζειν φήσομεν καθʼ Ἑλλάδα
sections.translation: But I will say it is not the custom in Hellas to bury those who have died at sea on the dry land .

sections.label: Section 1067
sections.original: τοῦτʼ αὖ κατορθοῖς · εἶτʼ ἐγὼ συμπλεύσομαι
sections.translation: You are setting this right , too ; then I will sail with you , and help let down the funeral offerings , in the same ship .

sections.label: Section 1069
sections.original: σὲ καὶ παρεῖναι δεῖ μάλιστα τούς τε σοὺς
sections.translation: You must above all be at hand ,

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.original: πλωτῆρας οἵπερ ἔφυγον ἐκ ναυαγίας .
sections.translation: with your sailors who escaped from the wreck .

sections.label: Section 1071
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἐάνπερ ναῦν ἐπʼ ἀγκύρας λάβω ,
sections.translation: Be sure that if I have a ship at anchor , they will be posted , man beside man , each with his sword .

sections.label: Section 1073
sections.original: σὲ χρὴ βραβεύειν πάντα · πόμπιμοι μόνον
sections.translation: You must direct everything ; only let there be winds in our sails to guide us , and a speedy ship .

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original: ἔσται · πόνους γὰρ δαίμονες παύσουσί μου .
sections.translation: It will be so ; for the deities will bring my troubles to an end . But from whom will you say that you heard I was dead ?

sections.label: Section 1077
sections.original: σοῦ · καὶ μόνος γε φάσκε διαφυγεῖν μόρον
sections.translation: From you ; allege that you were the only one to escape death , when you were sailing with the son of Atreus , and that you saw him dead .

sections.label: Section 1079
sections.original: καὶ μὴν τάδʼ ἀμφίβληστρα σώματος ῥάκη
sections.translation: It is true that these rags thrown around my body

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: ξυμμαρτυρήσει ναυτικῶν ἐρειπίων .
sections.translation: will bear witness with me as to the shipwreck .

sections.label: Section 1081
sections.original: ἐς καιρὸν ἦλθε , τότε δʼ ἄκαιρʼ ἀπώλλυτο ·
sections.translation: They have come at the right time , although then they seemed an ill-timed loss . Perhaps that misery may turn to good fortune .

sections.label: Section 1083
sections.original: πότερα δʼ ἐς οἴκους σοὶ συνεισελθεῖν με χρὴ
sections.translation: Should I to enter the house with you , or am I to sit quietly here at this tomb ?

sections.label: Section 1085
sections.original: αὐτοῦ μένʼ · ἢν γὰρ καί τι πλημμελές σε δρᾷ ,
sections.translation: Stay here ; for if he does something harsh to you , this tomb and your sword would protect you . But I will go into the house , cut off my hair , exchange my white robe for a dark one , and tear my cheek with nails that make the flesh bloody .

sections.label: Section 1090
sections.original: μέγας γὰρ ἁγών , καὶ βλέπω δύο ῥοπάς ·
sections.translation: For the contest is a great one , and I see two turns of the scale : either I must die if I am detected in my plot , or else come home and save you as well .

sections.label: Section 1093
sections.original: ὦ πότνιʼ ἣ Δίοισιν ἐν λέκτροις πίτνεις
sections.translation: Lady Hera , you who lie in the bed of Zeus , grant relief from their labors to two pitiable creatures ;

sections.label: Section 1095
sections.original: αἰτούμεθʼ ὀρθὰς ὠλένας πρὸς οὐρανὸν
sections.translation: we beg you , casting our arms straight towards heaven , where you have your home in an embroidery of stars . And you , who won the prize of beauty at the price of my marriage , Kypris , daughter of Dione , do not destroy me utterly . You have maltreated me enough before now ,

sections.label: Section 1100
sections.original: τοὔνομα παρασχοῦσʼ , οὐ τὸ σῶμʼ , ἐν βαρβάροις .
sections.translation: handing over my name , though not my body , to barbarians . Let me die , if you want to kill me , in my native land . Why are you so insatiable for mischief , practising arts of love , deceits , and treacherous schemes , and magic spells that bring bloodshed on families ?

sections.label: Section 1105
sections.original: εἰ δʼ ἦσθα μετρία , τἄλλα γʼ ἡδίστη θεῶν
sections.translation: If you were only moderate , in other ways you are by nature the sweetest of gods for men ; I don ’ t deny it . Exeunt Helen and Menelaos .

sections.label: Section 1.1107
sections.original: σὲ τὰν ἐναύλοις ὑπὸ δενδροκόμοις
sections.translation: Let me call on you , beneath leafy haunts , sitting in your place of song , you , the most sweetly singing bird ,

sections.label: Section 1.1110
sections.original: σὲ τὰν ἀοιδοτάταν ὄρνιθα μελῳδὸν
sections.translation: tearful nightingale , oh , come , trilling through your tawny throat , to aid me in my lament , as I sing the piteous woes of Helen and

sections.label: Section 1.1115
sections.original: τὸν Ἰλιάδων τʼ ἀει-
sections.translation: the tearful fate of Trojan women under the Achaeans ’ spears ; when he sped over the surging plains with foreign oar , when he came , came bringing to Priam ’ s race from Lacedaemon

sections.label: Section 1.1120
sections.original: σέθεν , ὦ Ἑλένα , Πάρις αἰνόγαμος
sections.translation: you , Helen , his unhappy bride — Paris , fatally wedded , under the guidance of Aphrodite .

sections.label: Section 1.1122
sections.original: πολλοὶ δʼ Ἀχαιῶν δορὶ καὶ πετρίναις
sections.translation: Many of the Achaeans have breathed out their last amid the spears and hurling stones and have gone to unhappy Hades ; their wives have cut off their hair in sorrow ,

sections.label: Section 1.1125
sections.original: ταλαινᾶν ἀλόχων κείραντες ἔθειραν ·
sections.translation: and their homes are left without a bride ; an Achaean man , who had only a single ship , lit a blazing beacon on sea-girt Euboia , and destroyed many of them , casting them onto the rocks of Kaphareus

sections.label: Section 1.1130
sections.original: Αἰγαίαις τʼ ἐνάλοις δόλιον
sections.translation: and the sea-shores of the Aegean , by the treacherous flame he kindled . The mountains of Malea provided no harbor , in the gusts of the storm , when Menelaos sped far away from his country , bearing on his ships a prize of the barbarian expedition , no prize but strife

sections.label: Section 1.1135
sections.original: Δαναῶν Μενέλας ἐπὶ ναυσὶν ἄγων ,
sections.translation: with the Danaans , Hera ’ s holy phantom .

sections.label: Section 2.1137
sections.original: ὅ τι θεὸς ἢ μὴ θεὸς ἢ τὸ μέσον ,
sections.translation: What is god , or what is not god , or what is in between — what mortal says he has found it by searching the farthest limit ,

sections.label: Section 2.1140
sections.original: ὃς τὰ θεῶν ἐσορᾷ
sections.translation: when he sees divine affairs leaping here and there again and back , in contradictory and unexpected chances ? You , Helen , are the daughter of Zeus ;

sections.label: Section 2.1145
sections.original: πτανὸς γὰρ ἐν κόλποις σε Λή-
sections.translation: for a winged father begot you in Leda ’ s womb ; and then you were proclaimed throughout Hellas , betrayer , faithless , lawless , godless . I do not know whatever certainty is among mortals ,

sections.label: Section 2.1150
sections.original: τὸ τῶν θεῶν δʼ ἔπος ἀλαθὲς ηὗρον .
sections.translation: but the word of the gods I have found true .

sections.label: Section 2.1151
sections.original: ἄφρονες ὅσοι τὰς ἀρετὰς πολέμῳ
sections.translation: You are fools , who try to win a reputation for virtue through war and marshalled lines of spears , senselessly putting an end to mortal troubles ;

sections.label: Section 2.1155
sections.original: εἰ γὰρ ἅμιλλα κρινεῖ νιν
sections.translation: for if a bloody quarrel is to decide it , strife will never leave off in the towns of men ; by it they won as their lot bed-chambers of Priam ’ s earth , when they could have set right by discussion

sections.label: Section 2.1160
sections.original: σὰν ἔριν , ὦ Ἑλένα .
sections.translation: the strife over you , O Helen . And now they are below in Hades ’ keeping , and fire has darted onto the walls like the bolt of Zeus , and you are bringing woe on woe . . . .

sections.label: Section 1165
sections.original: ὦ χαῖρε , πατρὸς μνῆμʼ · ἐπʼ ἐξόδοισι γὰρ
sections.translation: Greetings , tomb of my father ! For I buried you , Proteus , in the passageway so that I could address you ; and always as I leave and enter the house , I , your son Theoklymenos , call on you , father . You servants , take the hounds and hunting nets

sections.label: Section 1170
sections.original: δμῶες , κομίζετʼ ἐς δόμους τυραννικούς ·
sections.translation: into the palace . I have rebuked myself many times ; for do we not punish evil men with death ? And now I have heard that some Hellene has come openly to the land , without the guards ’ notice ,

sections.label: Section 1175
sections.original: ἤτοι κατόπτην ἢ κλοπαῖς θηρώμενον
sections.translation: either as a spy or thievishly hunting after Helen ; he will die if only I can catch him . Ah ! But it seems I have found everything in ruins ; for the daughter of Tyndareus has deserted her seat at the tomb and has been carried away from the land .

sections.label: Section 1180
sections.original: ὠή , χαλᾶτε κλῇθρα · λύεθʼ ἱππικὰ
sections.translation: Ho there ! undo the bars ; loose the horses from their stalls , servants , and bring out my chariot , so that the wife whom I long for may not be carried away from this land without my notice , for want of effort .

sections.label: Section 1184
sections.original: ἐπίσχετʼ · εἰσορῶ γὰρ οὓς διώκομεν
sections.translation: Wait ! for I see that the one I am pursuing

sections.label: Section 1185
sections.original: παρόντας ἐν δόμοισι κοὐ πεφευγότας .
sections.translation: is still in the house , and has not fled .

sections.label: Section 1186
sections.original: αὕτη , τί πέπλους μέλανας ἐξήψω χροὸς
sections.translation: You there , why have you put black robes instead of white on your body , and cut the hair from your noble head with a sword , and why do you drench your cheeks with pale tears ,

sections.label: Section 1190
sections.original: κλαίουσα ; πότερον ἐννύχοις πεπεισμένη
sections.translation: lamenting ? Do you mourn , persuaded by dreams in the night , or have you broken your heart with grief because you heard some voice within ?

sections.label: Section 1193
sections.original: ὦ δέσποτʼ — ἤδη γὰρ τόδʼ ὀνομάζω σʼ ἔπος —
sections.translation: My lord — for now I give you that name — I am destroyed ; everything of mine is gone and I am nothing .

sections.label: Section 1195
sections.original: ἐν τῷ δὲ κεῖσαι συμφορᾶς ; τίς ἡ τύχη ;
sections.translation: In what misfortune are you plunged ? What has happened ?

sections.label: Section 1196
sections.original: Μενέλαος — οἴμοι , πῶς φράσω ; — τέθνηκέ μοι .
sections.translation: Menelaos — alas , how shall I say it ?— is dead , my husband .

sections.label: Section 1197
sections.original: οὐδέν τι χαίρω σοῖς λόγοις , τὰ δʼ εὐτυχῶ .
sections.translation: I do not rejoice at your words , but it is good fortune for me . How do you know ? Did Theonoe tell you this ?

sections.label: Section 1199
sections.original: κείνη τε φησὶν ὅ τε παρὼν ὅτʼ ὤλλυτο .
sections.translation: Both she , and one who was there when he perished .

sections.label: Section 1200
sections.original: ἥκει γὰρ ὅστις καὶ τάδʼ ἀγγέλλει σαφῆ ;
sections.translation: Someone has come who announces this for certain ?

sections.label: Section 1201
sections.original: ἥκει · μόλοι γὰρ οἷ σφʼ ἐγὼ χρῄζω μολεῖν .
sections.translation: Someone has come ; and may he go where I want him to go !

sections.label: Section 1202
sections.original: τίς ἐστι ; ποῦ ʼστιν ; ἵνα σαφέστερον μάθω .
sections.translation: Who is it ? Where is he ? so that I may learn this more clearly .

sections.label: Section 1203
sections.original: ὅδʼ ὃς κάθηται τῷδʼ ὑποπτήξας τάφῳ .
sections.translation: That one , who is sitting crouched at this tomb .

sections.label: Section 1204
sections.original: Ἄπολλον , ὡς ἐσθῆτι δυσμόρφῳ πρέπει .
sections.translation: Apollo ! He certainly has unattractive clothing .

sections.label: Section 1205
sections.original: οἴμοι , δοκῶ μὲν κἀμὸν ὧδʼ ἔχειν πόσιν .
sections.translation: Alas ! I think my husband is in the same situation also .

sections.label: Section 1206
sections.original: ποδαπὸς δʼ ὅδʼ ἁνὴρ καὶ πόθεν κατέσχε γῆν ;
sections.translation: What is this man ’ s country , and where did he come from , to land here ?

sections.label: Section 1207
sections.original: Ἕλλην , Ἀχαιῶν εἷς ἐμῷ σύμπλους πόσει .
sections.translation: He is a Hellene , one of the Achaeans who saiIed with my husband .

sections.label: Section 1208
sections.original: θανάτῳ δὲ ποίῳ φησὶ Μενέλεων θανεῖν ;
sections.translation: What kind of death does he say Menelaos died ?

sections.label: Section 1209
sections.original: οἰκτρόταθʼ , ὑγροῖσιν ἐν κλυδωνίοις ἁλός .
sections.translation: The most piteous , in the watery waves at sea .

sections.label: Section 1210
sections.original: ποῦ βαρβάροισι πελάγεσιν ναυσθλούμενον ;
sections.translation: On what part of the barbarous ocean was he sailing ?

sections.label: Section 1211
sections.original: Λιβύης ἀλιμένοις ἐκπεσόντα πρὸς πέτραις .
sections.translation: He was cast up on the harborless rocks of Libya .

sections.label: Section 1212
sections.original: καὶ πῶς ὅδʼ οὐκ ὄλωλε κοινωνῶν πλάτης ;
sections.translation: How did this man not perish if he was sailing with him ?
